title: सावित्री गायत्री विस्तारित मन्त्र
devanagariText:
॥ श्री सावित्री गायत्री विस्तारित मन्त्र ॥

ॐ भूर्भुवः स्वः
तत्सवितुर्वरेण्यं
भर्गो देवस्य धीमहि
धियो यो नः प्रचोदयात्॥

॥ सावित्री ध्यानम् ॥
मुक्ताविद्रुमहेमनीलधवलच्छायैर्मुखैस्त्रीक्षणैः
युक्तामिन्दुनिबद्धरत्नमकुटां तत्त्वार्थवर्णात्मिकाम्।
गायत्रीं वरदाभयाङ्कुशकशाश्शूलं कपालं गुणं
शंखं चक्रमथारविन्दयुगलं हस्तैर्वहन्तीं भजे॥

॥ प्रातः सन्ध्या गायत्री ॥
ॐ भूर्भुवः स्वः तत्सवितुर्वरेण्यं
भर्गो देवस्य धीमहि धियो यो नः प्रचोदयात्।
ॐ आपो ज्योतीरसोऽमृतं ब्रह्म भूर्भुवः स्वरोम्॥

॥ मध्याह्न सन्ध्या गायत्री ॥
ॐ भूर्भुवः स्वः तत्सवितुर्वरेण्यं
भर्गो देवस्य धीमहि धियो यो नः प्रचोदयात्।
ॐ आपो ज्योतीरसोऽमृतं ब्रह्म भूर्भुवः स्वरोम्॥

॥ सायं सन्ध्या गायत्री ॥
ॐ भूर्भुवः स्वः तत्सवितुर्वरेण्यं
भर्गो देवस्य धीमहि धियो यो नः प्रचोदयात्।
ॐ आपो ज्योतीरसोऽमृतं ब्रह्म भूर्भुवः स्वरोम्॥

॥ गायत्री शीर्ष ॥
ॐ भूः। ॐ भुवः। ॐ स्वः। ॐ महः। ॐ जनः। ॐ तपः। ॐ सत्यम्।
ॐ तत्सवितुर्वरेण्यं भर्गो देवस्य धीमहि धियो यो नः प्रचोदयात्।
ॐ आपो ज्योतीरसोऽमृतं ब्रह्म भूर्भुवः स्वरोम्॥

॥ सप्तव्याहृति गायत्री ॥
ॐ भूः तत्सवितुर्वरेण्यम्।
ॐ भुवः भर्गो देवस्य धीमहि।
ॐ स्वः धियो यो नः प्रचोदयात्।
ॐ महः। ॐ जनः। ॐ तपः। ॐ सत्यम्॥

॥ फलश्रुति ॥
सावित्रीं यः पठेन्नित्यं सर्वपापैः प्रमुच्यते।
त्रिसन्ध्यं जपमानस्तु ब्रह्मलोकं स गच्छति॥

॥ इति श्री सावित्री गायत्री विस्तारित मन्त्र सम्पूर्णम् ॥
transliteration:
|| Shri Savitri Gayatri Extended Mantra ||

Om Bhur Bhuvah Svah
Tat Savitur Varenyam
Bhargo Devasya Dhimahi
Dhiyo yo nah prachodayat.

|| Savitri Dhyanam ||
Mukta-vidruma-hema-nila-dhavala-chchhayair-mukhaih-trikshaniah
Yuktam-indu-nibaddha-ratna-makutam Tattvartha-varnatmikam.
Gayatrim Varada-bhayankusha-kashas-shulam Kapalam Gunam
Shankham Chakram-atharavinda-yugalam Hastair-vahantim bhaje.

|| Pratah Sandhya Gayatri ||
Om Bhur Bhuvah Svah Tat Savitur Varenyam
Bhargo Devasya Dhimahi Dhiyo yo nah prachodayat.
Om Apo Jyotir-aso'mritam Brahma Bhur Bhuvah Svarom.

|| Madhyahna Sandhya Gayatri ||
Om Bhur Bhuvah Svah Tat Savitur Varenyam
Bhargo Devasya Dhimahi Dhiyo yo nah prachodayat.
Om Apo Jyotir-aso'mritam Brahma Bhur Bhuvah Svarom.

|| Sayam Sandhya Gayatri ||
Om Bhur Bhuvah Svah Tat Savitur Varenyam
Bhargo Devasya Dhimahi Dhiyo yo nah prachodayat.
Om Apo Jyotir-aso'mritam Brahma Bhur Bhuvah Svarom.

|| Gayatri Shirsha ||
Om Bhuh. Om Bhuvah. Om Svah. Om Mahah. Om Janah. Om Tapah. Om Satyam.
Om Tat Savitur Varenyam Bhargo Devasya Dhimahi Dhiyo yo nah prachodayat.
Om Apo Jyotir-aso'mritam Brahma Bhur Bhuvah Svarom.

|| Sapta-vyahriti Gayatri ||
Om Bhuh Tat Savitur Varenyam.
Om Bhuvah Bhargo Devasya Dhimahi.
Om Svah Dhiyo yo nah prachodayat.
Om Mahah. Om Janah. Om Tapah. Om Satyam.

|| Phalashruti ||
Savitrim yah pathen-nityam sarva-papaih pramuchyate.
Tri-sandhyam japamanastu Brahmalokam sa gachchhati.

|| Iti Shri Savitri Gayatri Vistarit Mantra Sampurnam ||
hindiMeaning: सावित्री गायत्री के विस्तारित रूप में प्रातः, मध्याह्न और सायं सन्ध्या के गायत्री मन्त्र, सप्तव्याहृति सहित गायत्री शीर्ष, और गायत्री देवी का ध्यान सम्मिलित है। मुक्ता-विद्रुम-हेम-नील-धवल वर्ण के मुखों वाली, त्रिनेत्रा, चन्द्रमणिजड़ित मुकुट वाली, वरद-अभय-अंकुश-कश-शूल-कपाल-गुण-शंख-चक्र-कमलयुगल धारिणी गायत्री देवी का ध्यान करें। जो नित्य सावित्री का पाठ करता है वह सर्वपापों से मुक्त होता है।
benefits: Complete extended Gayatri mantra with all three sandhya forms
Includes seven vyahritis and Gayatri Shirsha
Frees from all sins when recited daily
Leads to Brahmaloka when chanted at all three sandhyas
Most powerful Vedic mantra for spiritual illumination
description: Dedicated to Sun God Surya, the Savitri Gayatri Extended (सावित्री गायत्री विस्तारित मन्त्र) is a powerful Vedic meditation mantra revered in Hindu tradition. Drawn from Vedas, this text has been cherished by devotees for centuries. Regular recitation is believed to complete extended Gayatri mantra with all three sandhya forms, lead to Brahmaloka when chanted at all three sandhyas, and include seven vyahritis and Gayatri Shirsha. It is especially recommended on Sunday (Ravivar) for maximum spiritual benefit. The text contains 7 verses and takes around 10 minutes to recite, fitting conveniently into a daily spiritual routine. On this page, you can read the complete Savitri Gayatri Extended in Devanagari Sanskrit with English transliteration, Hindi meaning (arth), and free PDF download for offline recitation.
seoDescription: Read Savitri Gayatri Extended with all three Sandhya forms, Saptavyahriti and Gayatri Shirsha in Sanskrit with Hindi meaning.
alsoKnownAs: Extended Gayatri Mantra with Sapta Vyahriti

seoFaqs.question: What is the Savitri Gayatri Extended Mantra?
seoFaqs.answer: The Savitri Gayatri Extended Mantra is the complete, expanded form of the Vedic Gayatri Mantra used in the traditional Sandhya-vandana ritual. Beyond the familiar 24-syllable verse 'Tat Savitur Varenyam', it includes the Pranava (Om), the seven Vyahritis (Bhuh, Bhuvah, Svah, Mahah, Janah, Tapah, Satyam) representing the seven cosmic planes, the Gayatri Shirsha (head of Gayatri), the Sapta-Vyahriti Gayatri, the Dhyana shloka of Gayatri Devi, and the prayers for the three Sandhyas at sunrise, noon and sunset. This extended form is traditionally taught after Upanayana initiation.

seoFaqs.question: What is the source of the Extended Savitri Gayatri Mantra?
seoFaqs.answer: The core Gayatri verse is from the Rigveda (Mandala 3, Sukta 62, Mantra 10) and is attributed to the Vedic seer Vishvamitra. The extended components — the Vyahritis, Gayatri Shirsha, and Sandhya prayers — are drawn from the Yajurveda (Taittiriya Aranyaka), the Brahmana literature, and the Sandhya-vandana tradition. The compilation is preserved in classical Smarta tradition and elaborated in commentaries by Adi Shankaracharya and later Acharyas. It forms the central component of the daily Sandhya ritual prescribed for twice-born Hindus.

seoFaqs.question: What are the benefits of reciting the Extended Gayatri Mantra?
seoFaqs.answer: Daily recitation of the Extended Gayatri at the three Sandhyas is traditionally said to free the devotee from all sins, illuminate the intellect, and lead to Brahmaloka — the highest abode. The phalashruti (fruit-statement) declares 'Savitrim yah pathennityam sarva-papaih pramuchyate' — one who recites Savitri daily is freed from all sins. The Vyahritis are believed to purify the seven cosmic planes within the practitioner, while the Gayatri Shirsha grants spiritual illumination, mental clarity, and protection. It is regarded as the supreme Vedic mantra for spiritual evolution.

seoFaqs.question: When should the Savitri Gayatri Extended be recited?
seoFaqs.answer: The traditional time for recitation is Tri-Sandhya — the three sandhi periods of the day. Pratah Sandhya is performed before sunrise facing east, Madhyahna Sandhya at solar noon, and Sayam Sandhya at sunset facing west. Sunday (Ravivar) is especially auspicious as the day of Surya, the deity of Savitr. Many practitioners also chant it during Brahma Muhurta (about 96 minutes before sunrise). The Sapta-Vyahriti Gayatri portion is often added to elaborate forms of Sandhya-vandana and during Upakarma rituals like Avani Avittam.

seoFaqs.question: What is the meaning of the Gayatri Mantra?
seoFaqs.answer: The Gayatri verse means: 'Om — we meditate upon the supreme effulgence of Savitr, the divine Sun, the source of the three worlds (earth, atmosphere, heaven). May that divine light illumine and inspire our intellects.' The Vyahritis Bhuh-Bhuvah-Svah denote the three worlds, while Mahah, Janah, Tapah, Satyam denote the higher cosmic planes. The closing 'Apo jyoti raso amritam Brahma' invokes the elements as Brahman. Together, the extended form is a meditation on the Sun as the visible symbol of the formless Supreme Reality.

seoFaqs.question: Who is the deity invoked in the Extended Gayatri?
seoFaqs.answer: The mantra is addressed to Savitr — the solar deity in his role as the inspirer and life-giver, distinct from the Surya seen in the sky. Savitr represents the divine power behind the visible Sun, the source of consciousness and intellect. The presiding goddess of the mantra itself is Gayatri Devi, depicted in the Dhyana shloka as five-faced (representing the five elements and Pranava plus four Vedas), three-eyed, crowned, and holding a lotus, conch, discus, goad, whip, trident, skull, rosary, and abhaya-varada mudras. She personifies the mantra as Veda-Mata, the Mother of the Vedas.

seoFaqs.question: How many verses are in the Extended Savitri Gayatri text?
seoFaqs.answer: This compilation contains seven main sections: the core Gayatri verse, the Savitri Dhyanam, the Pratah Sandhya Gayatri, the Madhyahna Sandhya Gayatri, the Sayam Sandhya Gayatri, the Gayatri Shirsha (with seven Vyahritis), the Sapta-Vyahriti Gayatri, and the Phalashruti. Each Sandhya version closes with the formula 'Om Apo Jyoti raso amritam Brahma Bhur Bhuvah Svar Om'. The seven-verse format mirrors the seven Vyahritis, the seven cosmic planes, and the seven sages, giving the compilation its complete and self-contained Vedic structure.

seoFaqs.question: What language is the Savitri Gayatri Mantra in?
seoFaqs.answer: The text is in classical Vedic Sanskrit, the oldest stratum of the Sanskrit language preserved in the Rigveda. The page presents the mantra in Devanagari script with full English (IAST-style) transliteration to assist correct pronunciation, along with Hindi translations of each section. Vedic accents (svaras) — udatta, anudatta and svarita — are traditionally observed during recitation by trained chanters. For self-study, the Romanised transliteration helps maintain proper rhythm without altering the sacred sound-form.

seoFaqs.question: Can women recite the Extended Gayatri Mantra?
seoFaqs.answer: Modern teachers including Swami Vivekananda, Swami Dayananda Saraswati, and the Arya Samaj tradition have affirmed that women, like men, may chant the Gayatri Mantra. Historically the mantra was associated with Upanayana initiation given primarily to twice-born males, but reform movements and many contemporary lineages — including the Gayatri Pariwar founded by Pandit Shriram Sharma Acharya — have made the practice fully open to women, householders and seekers of all backgrounds. Sincere intent and reverent recitation are emphasised more than birth-criteria today.
